name: Chilled Beet Soup
recipeIngredient: rice wine vinegar or champagne vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the beet greens at the base of each beet.
2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil and add the beets. Reduce heat and cook, uncovered, at a low boil until tender (30-40 minutes). Test doneness by inserting a knife; if it goes in easily, they're ready. Drain and let cool or rinse under cold water quickly. Peel, cut into large pieces, and set aside.
3. Heat oil in a small saute pan over medium heat. Add the onion and 1/4 teaspoon salt; cook until tender (about 7 minutes). Add garlic and cook for an additional 2 minutes. Combine onion/garlic with beets in a blender or food processor and puree until smooth.
4. Gradually add stock, about 2 cups at a time, until desired consistency is reached. Be careful not to thin the soup too much.
5. Stir in orange juice, lemon juice (up to 3 teaspoons), vinegar, salt, and pepper. Adjust seasoning with more lemon juice, vinegar, salt, or pepper as needed.
6. Transfer soup to bowls, garnish each with a swirl of sour cream and a sprinkle of dill.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings
